Dear Mr. tttiller:
you_have reguested inforroal assistance with respect to the
mass mailing provisions of the political 
Refortr Act (the ,Actn).1

428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.o.Box 807 0 Sacramento CA 95804´0807 ● (916)322‐ 5660


our File No. I-90-13I
Page 2
(Ui11er Advice Letter No. A-88-257, copy enclosed). You have
subDitted portions of the 1990 sanple balIot panphlet and erish to
know if the use of the secretary of State's nane and signature is
stiI1 appropriate.
ANALYSIS
section 89001 of the Act prowides that "no nerrsletter or
other mass rnailinq shall be sent at public expense.rr A Dass mail-
ing is defined as tlro hundred or Dore substantially sinilar pieces
of nai1. 
(section 82041.5.) Regrulation l89ol2 clirifies 
that the
purpose of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using
pubJ.ic funds to send out newsletters and other mass nailings at
public expense. A mass Dailing may not be sent at public expense
if it contains the name, office, photograph or anv other reference
to an elected officer affiliated 
with the agency producinq or
sending the mailing and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer.
(ReguLation 18901 (a) . )
The regulation contains an exception for leqaI notices or
other items sent as required by law in which the use of the
elected officer's 
name, office, titfe, 
or signature is necessary
in the notice or other rnailing. 
(Regnrlation 18901(b) (7).) 
The
certificate of correctness required by Section 88005 (d) coDes
within this exception. we have reviewed the proposed format of
your certificate 
and bel-ieve that, with one exception, the use of
the Secretary of State's naBe and signature has been lj.nited to
what is reasonably required to be j-ncluded in such a certlficate.
The one excepti.on is that the Secretary of state's title 
should
not be included in the name of the certificate. 
This wil-L confinr
that, pursuant to our telePhone conversation of February 16, 1990,
you have now changed the certificate to read |tCertif icate of
correctness . rl
The portions of the sauple ba1lot provided contained the
titLe trsecretary of staterr in several other pl-aces. In our
teLephone conversation of February 16, those uses were discussed
as follows:
1. The title 
could be used in the Letterhead of the general
letter describing the contents of the panphlet and ernphasj.zing the
importance of voting. 
The regnrlation contains an exception
2 A revised versj.on of Regulation 18901 was adopted by the com-
nission on December 13, 1989, and a statutorily required public
conment period expired January 17, 1990. Although tbe revised
version ot ttre regulation is stil1 in the rulemaking process, it
represents the policy of the Connission. Therefore, we are advis-
ing agencies that they nay rely on it. 
The advice contained in
this letter is based upon the revised version of Regulation 18901,
a copy of which is encl-osed.


Our Fi■e NO. I-90-■38
. ‐  Page 3
pernitting use of the elected officer's name in the letterhead of
-the agency stationery- (section I89o1(b) (1) ')
2. The title cannot be used at the end of the letter in
place of a sigmature - (Section 18901(b) (1) ')
3. The title uust be removed frou the outside of the
pamphlet, except for the follolring:
a. It nay appear in the return address of the fom'
since the form- is i roaiter used in Place of an alrency
envelope. (Section 189o1(b) (1) ' )
b. It may apPear in the Postaqe ueter stamp, based upon
your representitiirir that this use is required by postal law'
lsection 18901(b) (7) . )
This lrill confirm that the nodificatlons provided to us in
.""o.d"rr"" with the above advice comply with the requirements of
Section 89ool and Regulation I89o1'
If you have any questions regarding the above ' you may
contact ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
